concept: Gathering / A Deep Flow of the Whole
description: The act of people coming together in a shared space and time. It is the creation of a temporary community, generating a collective energy and presence.
rationale: The word begins with 'tho', a gentle fricative that breathes collective presence into being—the sound of many voices softening into one shared hum. This flows into 'lu', a liquid consonant that pools and deepens, drawing individual streams into a common current. In just two syllables, the word captures gathering's essence: the breath of coming together ('tho') flowing into the depth of shared presence ('lu'). The fricative-to-liquid movement mirrors how separate beings merge into temporary wholeness, while the descending vowel pattern (o to u) draws energy inward and downward, grounding the collective in shared space and time.
